title: Constitution authority is not a shipping gate: human-activated policies suffice; the experiment track is optional evidence gathering
context: The G3/constitution experiment track (preregistrations, receipts, EXP-01/EXP-03) grew until it read as the main quest, blocking momentum and making day-to-day development feel bureaucratic. The owner reviewed what the ceremony actually guards and found only two things: (1) machine-minted rules auto-blocking merges with no human in the loop, and (2) public marketing claims backed by numbers. Neither gates shipping features, activating policies by hand, or normal development. EXP-01 is complete positive internal evidence; EXP-03 revision 2 is implemented and can run whenever a timed independent review is convenient.
decision: Treat explicit human activation as sufficient authority for blocking policies in this repo — the same standard as any lint or CI rule. The experiment track (EXP-03 revision 2 and beyond) continues only as optional evidence gathering for public claims and machine-autonomous authority; it is never a prerequisite for shipping features, releasing the VS Code extension, or activating a policy a human explicitly approves.
consequences: Development and releases proceed without waiting on experiment evidence.
Blocking policy activation requires exactly one explicit human approval, nothing more.
Public numeric claims (e.g. prevention rates) remain gated on completed preregistered experiments; the site keeps printing honest zeros until then.
Machine-autonomous rule minting with blocking authority remains off until the experiment track earns it.
EXP-03 revision 2 runs whenever the owner chooses; no timer or roadmap pressure attaches to it.
alternatives_rejected: Keep G3 sign-off as a prerequisite for all policy authority — rejected: it made self-imposed rigor a shipping bottleneck and the owner's own tooling no fun to use.
Drop the experiment track entirely — rejected: EXP-01's result is genuinely useful and public claims still need preregistered evidence to stay honest.
Grant machine-minted rules blocking authority now — rejected: a hallucinated rule silently blocking real work is the exact failure mode the constitution exists to prevent.
